description.value: Backalley doctors and mechanics of cyberware, using their skills to keep fellow edgerunners in good shape and cybered up, often at a price.
Increase Body by 2 (and related stats). Stacks. Max at 10 Body.

Intended as a direct substitute/improvement to regular armor, to compensate for ablation changes.

-1 to REF, DEX, and MOVE - these penalties do not stack with those for the helmet, and are removed by a BODY stat of 7 or higher (change the armor penalty stat in the item settings tab)..

Intended as a direct substitute/improvement to regular armor, to compensate for ablation changes.

-1 to REF, DEX, and MOVE - these penalties do not stack with those for the helmet, and are removed by a BODY stat of 7 or higher (change the armor penalty stat in the item settings tab)..
A shield with a bit more mass, used for riot control. Requires BODY 7 to wield effectively.
A soviet cybernetical replacement arm. Comes with a free hand pre-installed. -1 to rolls requiring arm without BODY 8+. (See Black Chrome pg. 20 for more details)
Armored knuckles which function as a Melee Weapon.
Anytime the stun baton deals damage, target must make an Endurance check: DV = 10+dmg. On failure, the target is stunned for their next turn.
AI Smartphone. +2 Library Search, +2 Wardrobe & Style depending on circumstance.
Briefcase used for hooking up a wounded person. Places them in stasis.
10h of light on a single charge. 100m/yd range.
Provides an LED showing vitals readouts. Can be linked to your agent.
Change eye color. Can be activated/deactivated at will.
Change eye color. Can be activated/deactivated at will.

information.notes:
Edwin is a medtech specialist who spent the better part of the last 15 years running with Militech, from the early 2030s up through to 2044. Hardly escaping unscathed, though, his damages came from the company rather than the front lines. Despite being the former wielder of a brute suit, clauses in his contract specified that certain experimental proprietary tech would be returned to the company - and in his case, that tech included his internal linear frame, his armor, his cyberarm, and would have taken the grafted muscle too except that the trauma of its removal likely would have killed him.

Left broke and without the gear he was expecting on the streets of NC, the Apothecary has turned to a career of mercwork nonetheless, with the corporate betrayal only being one more in a long line of swerves and crashes his life has taken. He's learning to contend with cyberpsychosis following the removal of so much of his previous gear, while also getting back on his feet and returning to work to maybe repurchase some of his old equipment. In his transition to mercwork, Edwin's become known for a tempered demeanor and personality that signals a man who knows he's running the edge of cyberpsychosis, but also knows how to handle it.

In the world beyond he takes pains to enjoy life's finer things, aware both of the health benefits and the essential nature they play in avoiding cyberpsychosis - a dissociative break is only one bad day away, and there's no cause to risk that by going to kibble instead of splurging for something better.

Difficulty: Easy - actions before words, keep your teammates moving, and don't be too worried about absorbing fire.

Style of Play: Methodical, powerful melee takedowns paired with high HP for tanking a key blow here or there, plus being able to shrug off an attack or two while saving your teammates. Equip your shield tactically, using it to absorb key blows rather than relying on it as a primary defense. Request to use BODY instead of DEX for athletics checks involving brute strength.

Note: Big Knucks/Stun Stick damage has been increased to reflect NC G&C Homebrew involving cyberware and high BODY scores. Ask your GM for clarification if you're uncertain!

Cyberpsychosis: Even for a merc, Edwin is particularly joyless and often quietly depressed, which is also effecting his memory. He lacks remorse for his actions, is often callous while speaking and acting, and often relies on medical stimulants to feel or act in any meaningful way.

Character Goals: Make a life for yourself after years being a gang lackey and years more working for a militant corporation. Indulge in the finer things and pursue therapy to retreat from cyberpsychosis.

Cyberware To Aspire To: Linear frame, a modern cyberarm, medscanner, toxin binders/nasal filters, popout shield… sanity allowing. Maybe get used to/recover from your current psychosis first, before doubling down.

In-Character Voice & Acting Inspiration: Captain Titus, Warhammer 40k; Wilhelm, Borderlands: The Pre-Sequel; Jasper, Steven Universe.

Local Expert: Watson (and/or NorCal Military Base at GM discretion)

Science: Medicine/physiology

Pharmaceuticals (recommended): Surge (for personal use) & Speedheal.

Shift Tacts: Electric blue, bright, and disorienting.

All edgerunners included within NCG&C are wholly original, made to be played out of the box, and created using the "Complete Package" rules found in RED's Core Rulebook.

transactions: Cyberarm and Big Knucks - GM Dan

lifepath.culturalOrigin: Born on the irradiated edge of the glass desert of the Middle East; parents migrated to NC when he was a toddler.
lifepath.personality: Tempered and even; calm, calculating, and surgical. Currently suffering from cyberpsychosis, taking these traits from odd to unsettling..
lifepath.clothingStyle: Urban flash; clean whites and yellows to signal his role as a medtech. The heavier, the better.
lifepath.valueMost: My body is a temple; it's my duty to bring it to it's highest potential.
lifepath.aboutPeople: Much easier to take apart physically than understand metaphorically.
lifepath.valuedPerson: Himself, but trying to find a partner who grounds him.
lifepath.valuedPossession: His musculature; removing it may very well render him comatose or just straight-out kill him.
lifepath.familyBackground: Parents were rendered refugees from the Mideast Meltdown, and chose to flee to Night City rather than Africa. Died poor and sick.
lifepath.childhoodEnvironment: Homeless and sick on the streets - raised by 6th Street and Scavs more than his parents. It takes a village.
lifepath.familyCrisis: Forced out of their home shortly after Edwin was born, his parents never managed to recover. Edwin's childhood illnesses from the migration broke his family, who died when he was 8, leaving him in the care of 6th Street.
lifepath.lifeGoals: Finally make a life for yourself after a hellish childhood and an abusive career inside Militech.
lifepath.tragicLoveAffairs: A short string of women, the next worst than the last; codependent in the very literal definition in many cases, sleeping together to avoid homelessness and to protect one another as much as for emotive reasons.
